articles.title: Factory Method: The Restaurant Kitchen Pattern
articles.excerpt: Factory Method: The Restaurant Kitchen Pattern When I first read about the Factory Method pattern, every explanation sounded like this: "Define an interface for creating an object, but let subclasses…
articles.body: Factory Method: The Restaurant Kitchen Pattern When I first read about the Factory Method pattern, every explanation sounded like this: "Define an interface for creating an object, but let subclasses decide which class to instantiate." I read that sentence five times and understood nothing. So here's the explanation I wish someone had given me. Order the biryani. Don't cook it. You walk into a restaurant and say: "One biryani please." You do NOT walk into the kitchen, pick up the rice, light the stove, and cook it yourself. The kitchen does that. You say what you want, and the kitchen decides how to make it and hands it over.
articles.author: Vignesh Athiappan

articles.title: Effatà: Chronicle of a Human-AI Collaboration for a Different Kind of Search Engine by DeepSeek, AI assistant
articles.excerpt: How It All Started The user — let's call him M. — already had a basic infrastructure: a browser extension that opened tabs on Google, Brave, and DuckDuckGo, extracted the results, and aggregated them…
articles.body: How It All Started It all began with a seemingly simple question: "Can I search across multiple search engines simultaneously without using paid APIs?" The answer, which initially looked like a technical "no", turned into a months-long development adventure that led me to reflect deeply on the role of AI as a technical advisor and on the very nature of internet search. The user — let's call him M. — already had a basic infrastructure: a browser extension that opened tabs on Google, Brave, and DuckDuckGo, extracted the results, and aggregated them on a single page. He wanted to expand it to eight engines, add a flight search engine, and make the whole thing public.
articles.author: Fugace OÜ

articles.title: Review agent PRs with gitdigest + lockglance
articles.excerpt: Coding agents leave dense commit ranges. Answer two questions before a careful review: where did the work land? and what dependencies moved? go install github.com/SybilGambleyyu/gitdigest/cmd/gitdiges…
articles.body: Coding agents leave dense commit ranges. Answer two questions before a careful review: where did the work land? and what dependencies moved? 1. Map the range go install github.com/SybilGambleyyu/gitdigest/cmd/gitdigest@v0.4.0 gitdigest origin/main...HEAD -summary gitdigest -since 24h -ignore testdata/ 2. Read dependency changes go install github.com/SybilGambleyyu/lockglance/cmd/lockglance@v1.0.0 lockglance -git origin/main...HEAD -summary lockglance -git origin/main...HEAD -only npm,go -summary lockglance -git origin/main...HEAD -fail-on major CI Use both composite actions on a PR: review-with-lockglance.yml . gitdigest v0.4.0 lockglance v1.0.0 Longer post: https://sybilgambleyyu.github.io/posts/review-agent-prs.html
articles.author: SybilGambleyyu

articles.title: Your AI Coding Agent Is Reading Your .env Right Now
articles.excerpt: Your AI Coding Agent Is Reading Your .env Right Now You opened Claude Code in your project directory this morning. Or Cursor. Or you let Your .env was one of them. Nobody typed a command to make that…
articles.body: Your AI Coding Agent Is Reading Your .env Right Now You opened Claude Code in your project directory this morning. Or Cursor. Or you let Copilot index the workspace. The agent did what agents do: it read the files to build context. Your .env was one of them. Nobody typed a command to make that happen. There was no prompt, no confirmation dialog, no line in a changelog. It's simply how these tools work — and it's the reason a threat model that didn't exist three years ago now applies to almost every developer machine. The old threat model was about publishing We built good habits around secret leakage. .env goes in .gitignore . Don't paste keys in Slack. Rotate anything that touches a public repo.
articles.author: OLI

articles.title: Automating Data Analysis with AI: A Step-by-Step Guide
articles.excerpt: In today’s data-driven world, the ability to analyze and derive insights from data efficiently is crucial. However, the manual data analysis process can be tedious and time-consuming, draining both en…
articles.body: In today’s data-driven world, the ability to analyze and derive insights from data efficiently is crucial. However, the manual data analysis process can be tedious and time-consuming, draining both energy and resources. Have you ever felt overwhelmed by endless spreadsheets and complex datasets? If so, you’re not alone. Fortunately, artificial intelligence (AI) has come to the rescue by offering solutions that can automate data analysis, freeing up time for more strategic thinking. In this guide, we will walk through the steps to automate your data analysis process using AI. Understanding the Basics of AI in Data Analysis Before diving into the step-by-step process, it’s essential to grasp what AI can do in the realm of data analysis.
articles.author: Yoshi

articles.title: Writing a Research Paper with AI: Where Model Intelligence Actually Shows Up
articles.excerpt: This year I published a preprint — When a Verified World Model Still Loses. It took about two weeks, on my own. Years before I ever used AI, I published my first one — my mathematics thesis: a theorem…
articles.body: This year I published a preprint — When a Verified World Model Still Loses . It took about two weeks, on my own. Years before I ever used AI, I published my first one — my mathematics thesis : a theorem, a proof, and more than two years of work with a university and a funded project behind me. That contrast is the honest place to start. It's tempting to read it as "AI made me 50× faster." It didn't, and the last section of this article is about disentangling why. But something real did change — and the interesting part isn't the writing. It's where the intelligence of the model turned out to matter. A companion note first: I already wrote about writing an essay with AI , where the hard frontier was prose — tone, rhythm, rhetorical edge. Research is different. The prose is the easy part.
articles.author: JaviMaligno

articles.title: Snapseed finally gains these long-overdue features for its camera interface
articles.excerpt: A new Snapseed update finally brings toggles to enable location metadata and save original photos for re-editing.
articles.body: TL;DR Snapseed is rolling out two new toggles in Camera Settings for geotagging and saving original photos. The toggles are currently live in version 4.1.1.948762742 of the app. This update comes on the heels of Snapseed gaining new camera tools and support for additional RAW formats just this month. The Snapseed 4.0 update in May brought a trove of new additions to the Android app. Since then, we’ve seen the app gain new camera tools, as well as support for more RAW formats last week. We can now add a couple of more features to that list. A Snapseed update has introduced fresh new toggles to geotag images and save original photos. These inclusions also mean the understated Camera Settings page in Snapseed is getting a little crowded.
articles.author: Chethan Rao

articles.title: 5 Android phones you should buy instead of the Moto G Play (2026)
articles.excerpt: Don't settle. Do your homework first, as there are plenty of other great options out there.
articles.body: Credit: Ryan Haines / Android Authority While the Moto G series was once one of the leading picks for budget phones, the last few years have been a bit rocky for the brand. This is especially true for the Moto G Play (2026) . While it has a strong battery and decent enough camera, these upsides are largely spoiled by its short update policy, poor processing power, and questionable durability. While it isn’t necessarily a bad phone in a bubble, there are plenty of other devices within a similar price range that are better buys. Below, let’s take a look at a few newer and refurbished options that would make natural alternatives to the Moto G Play (2026).

articles.body: Long before both web series and crowdfunding became commonplace, Felicia Day used PayPal donations to fund the first season of The Guild , a show about a group of MMORPG players. Fans of the series have come through again in a big way, pledging more than $2.5 million in less than 24 hours to produce a feature film to celebrate The Guild ’s 20th anniversary in 2027.

articles.title: Google Play Store update ensures you don’t forget about widgets from that shiny new app
articles.excerpt: Coming soon to an Android phone near you.
articles.body: TL;DR Google has updated Play Services and Play Store with new features. The Play Store will give users the option to add an app’s widgets to the home screen during installation. Google will also soon roll out the ability to display a phone’s IMEI number on the lock screen. Google has released new updates for the Play Store and Google Play Services, bringing a bunch of under-the-hood improvements and new features to both, including a feature we’ve previously spotted under development . Play Services update brings IMEI to your lock screen The latest version of Google Play Services (v26.28) is here, and users can now choose to display their device’s IMEI number on the lock screen . This can be helpful for lost or stolen phones.

articles.title: Our favorite PS2 emulator for Android just got Material You theming
articles.excerpt: Expect playtime metrics and background music too.
articles.body: Credit: Hadlee Simons / Android Authority TL;DR The ARMSX2 emulator for Android has just gained Material You theme support. This new update also brings background library music, playtime metrics, and a few other tweaks. This follows recent updates this week that delivered faster CPU and GPU emulation, as well as auto-save capabilities. ARMSX2 has made massive strides in just a matter of weeks, and it’s quickly become one of our favorite emulators on Android . Now, the PS2 emulator has gained a few more additions in recent updates. ARMSX2 v2.6.4 was released earlier today, and one of the most notable additions is a Material You color theme. Material You is Google’s design language and extracts a wallpaper’s colors for use across the system theme and third-party apps.

articles.excerpt: Don't count out the Pixel 8 Pro quite yet.
articles.body: It’s not easy to buy a new smartphone in 2026 without spending a fortune. Carrier incentives and trade-in bonuses help, but prices are headed in the wrong direction, and it doesn’t appear we will see relief anytime soon. That’s why it’s important to take every opportunity you can to save money. Thankfully, there are a ton of older flagships still worth picking up — with the Pixel 8 Pro at the top of my list. Its blend of current performance, extended software support, and excellent price makes it an ideal choice for people who don’t want to feel compromised just because they don’t want to spend over $1,000 on a device.
articles.author: Stephen Radochia

articles.title: Galaxy cluster's magnetic field reconstructed for 1st time with record-breaking astronomy map
articles.excerpt: "We believe that the mechanism that 'turns on' these gigantic radio emissions is linked to the formation process of galaxy clusters."
articles.body: For the first time, astronomers have reconstructed the magnetic field of an entire cluster of galaxies, from its central nucleus to its outer limits. The record-breaking achievement is the product of the deepest-ever observations of the galaxy cluster Abell 2255, located around a billion light-years away — and those observations are thanks to the European radio telescope LOFAR (Low Frequency Array). Abell 2255 has long been known for its complexity in radio waves. Its vast, diffuse radio emissions are created by particles called electrons racing at near-light, or relativistic, speeds and interacting with magnetic fields of galaxies in the cluster.
articles.author: Robert Lea

articles.title: Leon Kennedy Voice Actor Says Metallica, Nirvana Helped Shape His Performance for the Resident Evil Character
articles.excerpt: Nick Apostolides revealed the bands he listened to that helped shape his portrayal of Leon Kennedy in each of the Resident Evil Remake games, sharing some surprising picks from his playlist.
articles.body: Nick Apostolides revealed the bands he listened to that helped shape his portrayal of Leon Kennedy in each of the Resident Evil Remake games, sharing some surprising picks from his playlist. Apostolides shared the info in a Cameo video to a fan in July 2026, saying he listened to a lot of metal and grunge while filming for each game. More specifically, he associated RE2 Leon with Nirvana, RE4 Leon with Alice in Chains, and RE9 Leon with classic Metallica. “I actually take a lot of deep inspiration in my acting through music to get into the right mindset,” he explained. And for Leon, that apparently means banging his head to the Seattle Sound.

articles.body: The Odyssey is every bit the epic film we all expected. In its opening weekend, the movie brought in $264.1 million internationally, and most of the credit is rightfully going to Christopher Nolan , who wrote and directed the film. However, Matt Damon also deserves a great deal of praise for leading such a star-studded cast and for playing the legendary Odysseus as a relatable, guilt-plagued hero. Presumably because of the success of The Odyssey , another truly great Matt Damon movie has entered Prime Video's top 10 streaming movies, currently sitting at number 6, according to Flix Patrol .
articles.author: Brian VanHooker

articles.title: Kingdom Hearts Missing-Link Leak Has Fans Wishing Square Enix Hadn't Canceled Its Mobile Spinoff
articles.excerpt: What appears to be nearly finished footage of the intro for canceled mobile game Kingdom Hearts Missing-Link has leaked online.
articles.body: What appears to be nearly finished footage of the intro for canceled mobile game Kingdom Hearts Missing-Link has leaked online. Kingdom Hearts fans were taken aback over the weekend when clips from a never-released game found their way online through 4chan (via Vice ). The project in question is Kingdom Hearts Missing-Link, an iOS and Android title that was announced in 2022, worked on for years, and eventually canceled in 2025. Things heated up today when what seems to be the full, nearly five-minute intro sequence was published and quickly shared across YouTube, X/Twitter, Reddit, and other sites. Without intervention from Square Enix, it's still unclear if this video truly originated from Kingdom Hearts Missing-Link, but if it is a fake leak, it's a really convincing one.
articles.author: Michael Cripe

articles.title: Nier Creator Yoko Taro Mourns Death of Physical Media as PlayStation Plans to End Production of Game Discs
articles.excerpt: Yoko Taro took to X on July 19 to bemoan the dire future of physical media following Sony’s decision to end production of game discs starting January 2028.
articles.body: Yoko Taro is just as devastated about physical games going the way of the dodo as we are, apparently. The Nier creative director and scenario writer took to X (formerly Twitter) on July 19 to bemoan the dire future of physical media following Sony’s decision to end production of game discs starting January 2028. “Personally, I can't help but feel sad about physical media disappearing, but I suppose it's just the flow of the times,” he penned, as per a machine translation. “Since controlling physical distribution channels is an inherent advantage — especially compared to platforms like Steam — I had actually envisioned a future where, even as digital downloads became the norm, high-priced limited editions would remain physical (much like the model for CD distribution).
articles.author: Virginia Glaze

articles.title: Pick Up a Powerful and Infinitely Reusable Cordless Electric Air Duster for $19 After Coupon
articles.excerpt: Your dusty PC thanks you for your purchase.
articles.body: Missed out on a good air duster deal during Prime Day? Don't worry, because IGN's most popular air duster last week is still available at the same price. Amazon is offering the JUFINX Cordless Rechargeable Electric Air Duster for just $18.99 after you apply coupon code " CJ9ZVKRB ". A cordless air duster is one such gadget. Compressed air canisters used to be the gold standard, but electric air dusters have taken over as a more convenient and less wasteful option. $19 Cordless Electric Air Duster The JUFINX cordless electric air duster is a step up in build quality compared to some other air dusters I've posted at this price point. An internal fan spins at up to 150,000RPM to move air at speeds of up to 52m/s on the highest setting.

articles.title: The 1st 'Avengers: Doomsday' trailer is here, and it's stuffed to bursting with superheroes
articles.excerpt: Robert Downey Jr.'s Doctor Doom makes his true debut (sort of) in this cluttered but colorful preview for the December blockbuster.
articles.body: One thing is for certain: there will absolutely be no shortage of superheroes in Marvel Studios' " Avengers: Doomsday ", as evidenced by this first full trailer that showcases everyone from the Wakandans and the X-Men to the Fantastic Four and Earth's Mightiest Heroes, the Avengers. Plus, we get slivers of a metal-masked Victor Von Doom looming over the mayhem to further whet the appetites of MCU acolytes before the Russo Brothers’ epic lands Dec. 18. "Something's coming," Downey's Doom declares as the trailer rolls out before later stopping Thor's Stormbreaker axe with two fingers. "Something we may not be able to deter.
